data.description:
## Problem
Under concurrent multi-agent work, review-plan completes its (billed) LLM review but fails to persist the signature, raising PlanReviewGenerationChanged with before==after head sha. Reproduces whenever ANY other ticket in the store is written (comment/claim/transition) during the review window. Makes require_plan_review_for_claim unreliable in exactly the parallel epic/child decomposition rebar promotes.

## Root cause (confirmed by reading)
generation.sign_manifest aborts when fresh != initial_generation (generation.py:133). PlanReviewGeneration is a frozen dataclass whose equality compares ALL fields, including (a) ticket_store_revision (the STORE-WIDE tracker HEAD sha) and (b) relation_snapshot, whose type PlanRelationSnapshot (relation_snapshot.py:56) compares ticket_states_by_id — the full state of EVERY ticket in the store. initial_generation is captured at review START; fresh at sign time (minutes later). Any commit to any ticket in the window changes both fields, forcing PlanReviewGenerationChanged even when before==after within the attempt.

## Why it is a defect (not fail-closed by design)
The SIGNED manifest binds only own material + deps (file hashes) + related_material pins; the claim-time re-check compute_validity re-validates only those same subject/related scopes. The store-wide sign-time equality is therefore stricter than both what gets signed and what gets re-validated — it invalidates on differences that produce no change in the signed artifact.

## Fix
Scope the generation equality to the subject's own material + direct related material (+ phase/floor) — the same scope the manifest binds. Exclude ticket_store_revision and ticket_states_by_id from PlanReviewGeneration identity (e.g. field(compare=False) or a scoped __eq__). Keep the within-attempt before/after head fence (it correctly guards a concurrent COMMIT during collection) but stop comparing the minutes-old store-wide baseline.

## Acceptance criteria
- Signing SUCCEEDS when an UNRELATED ticket is committed to the tracker between review-start (initial_generation) and sign time, provided the subject's own material and its direct related material are unchanged.
- Signing still FAILS (PlanReviewGenerationChanged) when the SUBJECT's own material OR a direct child/prerequisite's material changed during the window.
- The within-attempt before/after head fence is preserved.
- A test simulates an unrelated concurrent tracker write during the window and asserts a signature is persisted; the pre-fix code path would raise PlanReviewGenerationChanged.

## Verification
pytest concurrency test: unrelated write during window -> signed; subject/related write during window -> PlanReviewGenerationChanged.
data.title: plan-review signing spuriously fails (PlanReviewGenerationChanged) under unrelated concurrent tracker writes
